DECLARATION OF JANE BRUNNER IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS REPLY TO DEFENDANTS OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO QUASH (TRANSACTION ID # 58298587) FILED BY PLAINTIFF DRAKEFORD SR, REGINALD L
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-06-2016 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT REGINALD DRAKEFORD'S MOTION TO QUASH DEFENDANTS' AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS' SUBPOENAS - THE MOTION IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOWS: (1) AS TO THE SUBPOENA TO 1ST UNITED SERVICES CREDIT UNION, THE MOTION IS MOOT AS DEFENDANT SERVED AN AMENDED SUBPOENA TO WHICH PLAINTIFF WILL NOT OPPOSE. REPLY MPA, 2:7-10. (2) AS TO THE SUBPOENAS TO LISA JOHNSON-WRIGHT, PHD.; DR. CECIL LEE; AND THE CUSTODIAN OF RECORDS AT THE VA CLINIC, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF HAS PLACED HIS MENTAL STATE AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE IN THE VERIFIED COMPLAINT BY ALLEGING SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND IN DISCOVERY RESPONSES INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO THE 6 SERIES OF THE FORM INTERROGATORIES. HOWEVER, DEFENDANT'S SUBPOENAS ARE OVERLY BROAD. THE SUBPOENAS ARE LIMITED TO THE PREVIOUS FIVE YEARS AND ARE LIMITED TO RECORDS RELATED TO "EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, STRESS, DIFFICULTY SLEEPING, HIGH ANXIETY, AND DEPRESSION." (SEE PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 6.3(A)). THE COURT SET A DEADLINE OF 30 DAYS TO PRODUCE RECORDS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
Off Calendar May-17-2016 Continued To Jul-01-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Plaintiff And Cross-Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defendants And Cross-Complainants Subpoenas
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jane Brunner of Siegel & Yee telephone number 510-839-1200 for Plaintiff.
Gilbert Castro of Littler Mendelson, P.C. telephone number 415-439-6210 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling with one addition as follows: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT REGINALD DRAKEFORD'S Motion To Quash Defendants' And Cross-Complainants' Subpoenas - The Motion is GRANTED and DENIED as follows: (1) As to the subpoena to 1st United Services Credit Union, the Motion is MOOT as defendant served an amended subpoena to which plaintiff will not oppose. Reply MPA, 2:7-10. (2) As to the subpoenas to Lisa Johnson-Wright, PhD.; Dr. Cecil Lee; and the Custodian of Records at the VA Clinic, the Court rules as follows: plaintiff has placed his mental state at issue in this case in the verified Complaint by alleging severe emotional distress and in discovery responses including, but not limited to, plaintiff's response to the 6 Series of the Form Interrogatories. However, defendant's subpoenas are overly broad. The subpoenas are limited to the previous five years and are limited to records related to "emotional distress, stress, difficulty sleeping, high anxiety, and depression." (See plaintiff's response to Form Interrogatory 6.3(a)). The Court set a deadline of 30 days to produce records. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)
